sessions.intent: Design Data envelope architecture — Engine.Types, Type context, Data restructure, envelope pipeline

before.assumptions: Type knowledge scattered across three static/instance sources. Data has no context. Type is eagerly derived. No transport serialization view exists.
before.risk: Large architectural change touching Data (universal type), Type, and TypeMapping. Phased approach mitigates — each phase independently shippable.

sessions.plan: Four-phase design: (1) Engine.Types replaces static TypeMapping, (2) Type gets context + lazy derivation, Data gets late-bound context, (3) Data partial class split + Out view, (4) Envelope pipeline with Wrap/Compress/Encrypt chain. OBP throughout.

actions.context: Moved handover docs from Documentation/Runtime2/architect-handover/ to .bot/
Design spec and summaries
OBP learnings from design session

after.status: Design complete. Four-phase plan ready for coder handoff.
after.health: Good. No code changes, pure design.
after.notes: Coder should read plan.md + learnings.md before starting Phase 1.

sessions.intent: Implement Phase 1: Engine.Types — consolidate scattered type knowledge into a single live instance on Engine

before.assumptions: Type knowledge is scattered across static TypeMapping (Engine/Utility), FileModule.TypeMapping (instance), and MimeTypeHelper (Utils). Data.cs, file/types.cs, and StepActions reference static TypeMapping directly.
before.risk: Phase 1 is additive — no existing behavior changes. Risk is low.

sessions.plan: Create PLang/Runtime2/Engine/Types/this.cs with all type knowledge. Add Types property to Engine. Add EngineTypes global alias. Write 62 TUnit tests. Keep old static TypeMapping as-is until Phase 2.

actions.context: New @this class consolidating PLang name<->CLR type, extension->Kind, extension->MIME, Kind->compressibility.
Added Types property to Engine, EngineTypes global alias.
62 TUnit tests covering all public methods.

after.status: Phase 1 complete. Engine.Types created with full type knowledge. 62 tests pass.

sessions.intent: Test quality analysis of coder v1

before.assumptions: 62 tests for new class, likely happy path only.

sessions.plan: Run tests, analyze for intent vs implementation, check edge cases.

actions.context: 8 findings.

sessions.intent: Test quality analysis of coder v2 — Phase 2 context + lazy derivation

before.assumptions: v1 findings likely not addressed yet.

sessions.plan: Run tests, verify v1 carry-forward, analyze Phase 2 quality.

actions.context: Critical Add→KindOf bug found.

sessions.intent: Verify v2 fixes + Phase 3 test quality

before.assumptions: Coder fixed v2 findings and implemented Phase 3.

sessions.plan: Run tests, verify fixes, analyze Phase 3.

actions.context: All fixes verified. Verdict: approved.

sessions.intent: Test quality analysis of Phase 4 envelope pipeline

before.assumptions: Pipeline involves serialization and compression — failure modes at transport boundary.

sessions.plan: Analyze pipeline tests, check error paths.

actions.context: Critical: Decompress no exception handling. Verdict: needs-fixes.

after.status: Needs fixes. 1366 tests pass but Decompress will crash on corrupt data.

actions.context: All fixes verified. Verdict: approved.

after.status: Approved. 1372 tests pass. Ready for auditor.

sessions.intent: Full code review of data-envelope-architecture Phases 1-4

before.assumptions: Tester v5 approved, 1372 tests pass. Code should be functionally correct. Review focuses on OBP compliance, thread safety, contract fidelity, and ripple impact.
before.risk: Data is the universal type — any issue in Data.cs affects every module. Engine.Types is shared singleton — thread safety is critical.

sessions.plan: Read all production code and tests. Verify tests pass independently. Check OBP compliance, thread safety, error handling conventions, serialization correctness. Write auditor-report.json with numbered findings.

actions.context: Full review of all changed production files. Found 8 findings: 2 major (thread safety, temporal coupling), 3 minor (ServiceError, double-navigation, ContainsValue), 3 nit.
Test quality verified independently. 1372 pass. Adequate coverage for all phases. Gap: error key assertions after ServiceError fix.
8 findings written. Approved with fixes recommended.

after.status: Review complete. 8 findings in auditor-report.json. Approved with fixes recommended.
after.health: Good overall. Thread safety (finding #1) is the priority fix. Code is clean, well-structured, and OBP-compliant.
after.notes: Two major findings should be addressed before merge. Thread safety in Engine.Types is latent but becomes real with concurrent contexts. RehydrateNestedData temporal coupling is fragile but functional.

sessions.intent: Verify auditor fix commit + deep test quality analysis of fixes themselves

before.assumptions: Coder fixed all 8 auditor findings. Code changes are correct structurally. Question is whether the fixes themselves have test coverage.
before.risk: Safety features (zip bomb, thread safety) added without regression tests.

sessions.plan: Run full test suite, verify each auditor fix in code, analyze whether new code paths are tested, identify false greens where fixes exist in code but not in tests.

actions.context: Verified all 8 auditor fixes correct in code. Found 1 critical gap (zip bomb untested), 1 major gap (thread safety untested), 2 minor gaps (StatusCode, inner context). Verdict: needs-fixes.

after.status: Review complete. 4 findings in test-report.json. Verdict: needs-fixes.
after.health: Code is correct but two security-critical features lack regression tests.
after.notes: Critical: zip bomb protection (MaxDecompressedSize) has zero test coverage. Major: ConcurrentDictionary/lock has no concurrent test.

sessions.intent: Security audit of data-envelope-architecture — blue team + red team analysis of Data envelope pipeline, navigation, type registry, and memory stack

before.assumptions: Auditor found 8 functional findings but did not perform adversarial security analysis. Code handles untrusted data at transport boundaries (Decompress). Zip bomb mitigation was added. No other security-focused review has been done.
before.risk: Data is the universal type. Navigation, envelope pipeline, and type resolution all process potentially untrusted input. Stack overflow, type confusion, and reflection-based exfiltration are primary concerns.

sessions.plan: Phase 1: Blue team defensive audit — map attack surface for Data.cs (all partials), MemoryStack, Engine.Types, ValueNavigators. Phase 2: Red team offensive testing — construct exploit scenarios for stack overflow via recursion, zip bomb edge cases, reflection exfiltration, variable resolution loops, type registry poisoning. Phase 3: Record findings in security-report.json and learnings.

actions.context: Blue team: mapped 6 attack surface areas. Red team: identified 12 findings (4 high, 6 medium, 2 low). Primary pattern: unbounded recursion in 5 locations.
Security report, detailed findings, summaries, and learnings.

after.status: Security audit complete. 12 findings in security-report.json. 4 HIGH: unbounded recursion (x3) + system variable injection. 6 MEDIUM: reflection exposure, JSON size limits, variable resolution cycles, namespace spoofing, unimplemented crypto, recursive type parsing. 2 LOW: merge growth, deepclone limits.
after.health: Code has good defensive intent (gzip bomb protection, ServiceError, try/catch on decompress) but incomplete boundary hardening. Recommend addressing HIGH findings before merge.
after.notes: The recurring pattern is unbounded recursion — same fix applies everywhere (add depth parameter). System variable injection is an architectural gap in MemoryStack.Set(). ObjectNavigator reflection is a capability leak by design.

sessions.intent: Update security report after creator review — correct threat model, reclassify findings

before.assumptions: v1 applied a traditional security model (defend against the user). Creator corrected: PLang is user-sovereign, .pr files are trusted, trust boundary is cryptographic signatures.
before.risk: Incorrect threat model leads to wasted effort on non-issues and missed focus on real boundaries.

sessions.plan: Reclassify #4/#5 as by-design, upgrade #9 to HIGH (Verified is the trust boundary), downgrade #8 to LOW, update all deliverables.

actions.context: Updated threat model, reclassified 4 findings, added threat_model field.
Updated all deliverables with corrected threat model and reclassified findings.

after.status: v2 complete. Threat model corrected. Priority: #9 (Verified integrity) > #1-3 (recursion at transport) > #7/#10 (recursion elsewhere).
after.health: Findings properly calibrated to PLang's actual security model.
after.notes: Key insight: don't flag user-sovereign actions as vulnerabilities. Focus on transport boundaries and signature integrity.

sessions.intent: Fix unbounded recursion (5 locations), dedup fromJson, Verified private set, add missing tests

before.assumptions: Security audit found 5 unbounded recursion DoS vectors, auditor found zip bomb test gap, tester found Merge test gap. User rejected #4 and #5 as by-design.
before.risk: Medium — depth limits are mechanical but Clr() fallback change could affect type resolution. InternalsVisibleTo is new for this project.

sessions.plan: 1. Add depth params to all 5 recursive methods. 2. Cycle detection for ResolveVariablesInPath. 3. Dedup fromJson. 4. Verified private set. 5. Tests for all changes.

actions.context: Depth limits on all 5 recursive methods. ResolveVariablesInPath uses HashSet cycle detection instead of depth counter.
Deleted duplicate UnwrapJsonElement, now calls Data.UnwrapJsonElement (internal static).
Added InternalsVisibleTo for PLang.Tests.
12 new tests: depth limits (3), zip bomb (1), StatusCode (4), Merge (3), Verified (1 new + 2 updated).

after.status: All 5 recursion DoS vectors fixed. 1384 tests pass (12 new). Ready for review.
after.health: Green. All security HIGH findings addressed except #4 (by design).
after.notes: Clr() behavior change: list<unknownType> now returns null instead of List<object>. No existing tests depended on old behavior.

sessions.intent: Run and commit PLang integration tests for data-envelope-architecture features

before.assumptions: 3 PLang test suites created with hand-written .pr files in previous session. Need to verify they pass and commit.
before.risk: Low — tests only, no production code changes.

sessions.plan: Build PlangConsole, run all PLang tests with !test flag, verify 3 new suites pass, commit.

actions.context: 3 new PLang test suites verified and committed. All 17 PLang tests pass (14 existing + 3 new).

after.status: Complete. 17/17 PLang integration tests pass. Committed to data-envelope-architecture.
after.notes: Hand-written .pr files work correctly. Test runner only needs *.test.pr — app.pr and start.pr are optional.

sessions.intent: Code simplicity analysis of data-envelope-architecture branch changes — OBP compliance, simplification, readability

before.assumptions: Branch has been through coder, tester, auditor, and security review cycles. Code is functionally correct with 1384 tests passing. Previous reviews focused on correctness/security, not OBP and simplicity.
before.risk: Low — analysis only, no code changes.

sessions.plan: Analyze all 17 changed files in data-envelope-architecture branch. Three-pass: OBP compliance, simplification, readability. Production code gets full analysis, test code gets lighter readability pass.

actions.context: Full 3-pass analysis of all production files. 10 CLEAN, 2 NEEDS WORK (minor). No OBP violations.
Readability + pattern consistency pass. 3 CLEAN, 1 NEEDS WORK (minor — test name doesn't match what it tests).
Analysis report, session summary, cross-session summary.

after.status: Analysis complete. 12 CLEAN, 3 NEEDS WORK (minor). No OBP violations. 7 findings (1 medium, 5 low, 1 info).
after.health: Code is well-structured and OBP-compliant. Minor duplication and consistency issues.
after.notes: Most actionable finding: duplicated CleanName in Data.cs and MemoryStack.cs.

sessions.intent: Test quality analysis of coder v5 security hardening — depth limits, cycle detection, fromJson dedup, Verified private set

before.assumptions: Coder v5 addresses security audit + tester v6 findings. 12 new tests. Should resolve zip bomb, Merge, StatusCode gaps.
before.risk: New security features (cycle detection, depth limits) may be untested at integration level.

sessions.plan: Run full test suite. Verify v6 findings resolved. Hunt for gaps in new security code — especially cycle detection and depth limit integration paths.

actions.context: 3 of 6 v6 findings resolved. Found 2 new major gaps: cycle detection untested, GetChild depth error not tested through MemoryStack. Plus 2 minor. Thread safety carry-forward.

after.status: Review complete. 4 new findings + 1 carry-forward in test-report.json. Verdict: needs-fixes.
after.health: Security hardening code is correct. But two security features have zero test coverage at the integration level.
after.notes: Pattern: depth limits tested in isolation but not through callers. Cycle detection entirely untested.

sessions.intent: Higher-level review of coder v5 + tester v7 — applying learnings from runtime2-settings to find cross-concern gaps

before.assumptions: Coder v5 added depth limits, cycle detection, fromJson dedup, Verified → private set. Tester v7 found cycle detection untested and GetChild→MemoryStack integration gap. My v1 analysis was file-by-file. Applying new learnings: trace data origins, check clone/copy families, review fixes against full type surface.

sessions.plan: Read all changed production files + tests. Apply learnings: trace JSON data through full lifecycle, audit clone/copy methods as a family, check every code path for test coverage, review Cast/Convert patterns against full type surface.

actions.context: Higher-level cross-concern analysis. Found: (1) decimal precision loss in UnwrapJsonElement, (2) MemoryStack.Clone doesn't propagate Context, (3) GetChild→MemoryStack contract change untested (tester v7 carry-forward), (4) fromJson masks depth error as JsonParseError. Added 4 todos.
4 new todos, 6 learnings from applying runtime2-settings insights to this branch.

after.status: Higher-level review complete. 4 new todos added. 6 learnings recorded. Key insight: JSON number precision loss (decimal→double) is same class of bug as Settings int→long.
after.health: Code is structurally sound but has cross-concern gaps that file-by-file review missed.
after.notes: Clone methods are a recurring blind spot — both branches have the same pattern of Clone not propagating new properties.

sessions.intent: Fix 4 cross-concern gaps from code analyzer higher-level review

before.assumptions: Code analyzer found: decimal precision loss, Clone missing Context, MemoryStack.Get depth contract, fromJson masking depth error.
before.risk: Low-medium — decimal change affects all JSON number parsing. Clone context propagation changes a pre-existing test.

sessions.plan: 1. UnwrapJsonNumber: TryGetInt64→TryGetDecimal→GetDouble. 2. MemoryStack.Clone sets Context. 3. fromJson: separate InvalidOperationException catch. 4. Tests for all.

actions.context: Decimal precision via UnwrapJsonNumber, Clone context propagation, separate depth error catch in fromJson.
7 new tests + 1 updated. Decimal precision, integer type, clone context, depth navigation, fromJson depth/decimal.

after.status: All 4 analyzer findings fixed. 1390 tests pass, 0 failures.
after.notes: JSON 19.99 now stays decimal. Clone_DataHasNoContext updated to Clone_PreservesDataContext.
